feed.title: Miranda Lab
feed.subtitle: We develop machine learning methods to understand cellular behavior and interactions across imaging and omics data. Our research focuses on integrating diverse biological modalities to model, simulate, and predict complex biological processes, with applications in disease diagnosis and treatment.

entry.title: Digital Futures Flagship Project Awarded
entry.content:
We are excited to announce that a Digital Futures Flagship project, AI-Based 3D Spatial Cancer Biology, has been awarded to Gisele Miranda, Jens Lagergren, and Joakim Lundeberg. The project brings together expertise in artificial intelligence, computational modeling, spatial omics, and cancer biology to develop new methods for studying tumors as three-dimensional biological systems.

While current spatial transcriptomics technologies provide detailed molecular information from two-dimensional tissue sections, our goal is to use AI to reconstruct and analyze the three-dimensional organization of tumors and their microenvironments.

By integrating cutting-edge AI with state-of-the-art spatial omics technologies, this interdisciplinary collaboration aims to establish a new computational framework for studying cancer biology in three dimensions, opening new opportunities for precision oncology and data-driven biomedical discovery.

We thank Digital Futures for their support and look forward to sharing the scientific advances that will emerge from this exciting initiative.
entry.summary: We are excited to announce that a Digital Futures Flagship project, AI-Based 3D Spatial Cancer Biology, has been awarded to Gisele Miranda, Jens Lagergren, and Joakim Lundeberg. The project brings together expertise in artificial intelligence, computational modeling, spatial omics, and cancer biology to develop new methods for studying tumors as three-dimensional biological systems.

entry.title: PALS Grant Awarded to Gisele Miranda and Avlant Nilsson

link.title: PALS Grant Awarded to Gisele Miranda and Avlant Nilsson

entry.content:
Gisele Miranda and Avlant Nilsson (Karolinska Institutet - KI) have been awarded a grant through the Program for Academic Leaders in Life Science (PALS). PALS is a collaboration between the DDLS, SciLifeLab, and WCMM Fellow programs, co-funded by the Knut and Alice Wallenberg Foundation, with the goal of fostering new collaborations among early-career research leaders in Sweden.

The funded project, Linking Omics and Images with Deep Learning to Decode Cellular Responses, combines the complementary expertise of the two groups to better understand how genetic perturbations shape cellular behavior. The project will integrate image-based profiling and omics-based modeling through deep learning, creating a framework that links molecular signaling networks with cellular phenotypes. By combining high-content microscopy data with molecular measurements, the project aims to uncover mechanistic relationships between cellular state and morphology in PDAC.

We are grateful to PALS and the Knut and Alice Wallenberg Foundation for their support and look forward to strengthening this exciting collaboration between groups at KTH and KI.
entry.summary: Gisele Miranda and Avlant Nilsson (Karolinska Institutet - KI) have been awarded a grant through the Program for Academic Leaders in Life Science (PALS). PALS is a collaboration between the DDLS, SciLifeLab, and WCMM Fellow programs, co-funded by the Knut and Alice Wallenberg Foundation, with the goal of fostering new collaborations among early-career research leaders in Sweden.

entry.title: Digital Futures Demonstrator Project Awarded
entry.content:
A Digital Futures Demonstrator project has been awarded to Hans Blom and Gisele Miranda.

The project brings together expertise in advanced microscopy, artificial intelligence, and kidney pathology to develop a next-generation precision medicine pipeline for quantitative disease assessment. A central part of the project will be carried out by David Unnersjö Jess and Jen-Hung Wang, who will work on the implementation and development of the imaging and machine learning components of the pipeline.

The project will explore state-of-the-art self-supervised learning approaches to automatically identify, segment, and quantify disease-relevant structures from large-scale 3D microscopy data. By combining high-resolution imaging of intact kidney biopsies with AI-assisted image analysis, the project aims to improve diagnostic precision while reducing the time and effort required for pathology workflows.

We thank Digital Futures for their support and look forward to sharing the progress and outcomes of this exciting initiative.
entry.summary: A Digital Futures Demonstrator project has been awarded to Hans Blom and Gisele Miranda.

entry.title: RDCP Project Approved
entry.content:
We are excited to announce the approval of a new Research Data Community Project (RDCP), supported by SciLifeLab and the Wallenberg National Program for Data-Driven Life Science.

Project title: Microscopy Foundation Model & AI-Ready Data Hub for Smart Microscopy

The initiative is led by our collaborator Wei Ouyang and brings together researchers from across the SciLifeLab community to advance the next generation of AI-driven microscopy.

Gisele Miranda is one of the consortium PIs. She will contribute with expertise in machine learning and image analysis as part of this interdisciplinary effort.

The project aims to build an AI-ready microscopy data hub as a foundation for training robust models capable of interpreting samples in real time and guiding microscopy acquisition on the fly.
entry.summary: We are excited to announce the approval of a new Research Data Community Project (RDCP), supported by SciLifeLab and the Wallenberg National Program for Data-Driven Life Science.

entry.title: Maximilian Senftleben joins BIIF
entry.content:
We are pleased to welcome Maximilian Senftleben to the BioImage Informatics Facility (BIIF) as a Bioimage Analyst.

Max will contribute to the implementation, development, and dissemination of state-of-the-art image analysis workflows for the life science community. His work will support researchers across a wide range of imaging modalities and biological applications, helping to advance reproducible and scalable image-based research.

We are excited to have Maximilian join the team and look forward to his contributions to the facility and the broader imaging community.
entry.summary: We are pleased to welcome Maximilian Senftleben to the BioImage Informatics Facility (BIIF) as a Bioimage Analyst.

entry.title: New PhD students
entry.content:
We are delighted to welcome Jen-Hung Wang and Felipe Colombelli as PhD students in the lab.

Jen-Hung’s research focuses on image-based profiling and computational approaches to understanding cellular phenotypes, while Felipe works on machine learning methods for integrating multimodal biological data.
entry.summary: We are delighted to welcome Jen-Hung Wang and Felipe Colombelli as PhD students in the lab.

entry.title: MirandaLab is Established
entry.content: The Miranda Lab was officially established in September 2024. The lab focuses on developing machine learning methods for computational biology, with particular interests in image-based profiling, multimodal learning, and AI-driven biological discovery.
entry.summary: The Miranda Lab was officially established in September 2024. The lab focuses on developing machine learning methods for computational biology, with particular interests in image-based profiling, multimodal learning, and AI-driven biological discovery.
